text:
Once upon a time in a tranquil forest far away, there lived a wise old owl. His favorite spot was nestled among ancient oaks, where the sunlight filtered through ancient leaves.

One dewy morning, the wise old owl heard a tiny voice crying out. Peeping down from a branch, he discovered a fledgling bird stranded on the soft moss. Starving birds never dared swoop such things, but the little one was brave.

With a gentle swoop, the owl carried the fledgling home. That evening, under twinkling stars, they dined together. The owl regaled the bird with tales of faraway lands. Every story was a new world.

With time, their friendship grew. The fledgling learned about courage, about friendship, about stars—one bright one for the owl.

Years passed. The owl couldn't fly anymore; his wings were pale. Yet, he loved watching the fledgling soar. Together they watched the moon and stars.

One gray
